title: Zigzag Level Order Traversal
description:
### Objective
Return level order traversal of a binary tree, but reverse the node order on every other level to produce a zigzag pattern. The root level is left-to-right, the next is right-to-left, and so on. Use a queue and avoid extra per-node allocations to keep it fast for typing practice.

### Function Contract
Implement `__init__(self: any, val: any, left: any, right: any)` in Python.
Do not change input/output semantics expected by the tests.

### Input and Output
- Input `tree`: type `array` (example: `[3,9,20,null,null,15,7]`).
- Return type: `array`.

### Requirements
- Return the exact expected output for every test case.
- Handle edge cases (empty input, duplicates, boundary values) without raising exceptions.
- Target complexity: time `O(n)`, space `O(w)`.

### Constraints
- 0 ≤ nodes ≤ 10^5
- Node values are 32-bit signed
explanation:
Intuition
BFS groups nodes by level. Reversing every other level yields the zigzag without altering child enqueue order. A boolean flag toggles the direction after each level.

Approach
1. Initial Setup: Initialize a deque with root if present.
2. Main Algorithm: Process level by fixed size, append children, reverse list when direction is right-to-left.
3. Return Result: Append level to result and flip the flag.

Complexity Analysis
- Time Complexity: O(n)
- Space Complexity: O(w) where w is max width

Key Insights
- Direction flag avoids complex double-ended logic
- Level-size snapshot preserves boundaries

Common Pitfalls
- Reversing the queue instead of the level list is wasteful
- Forgetting to toggle flag breaks zigzag pattern
hints: Reverse the collected level on odd depths
